atcddd 0.2.0

Get direct children of an ATC code in the hierarchy

Source: R/lookup.R
atc_children.Rd

Returns all ATC codes that are immediate children of the given parent code. This works offline using any data frame of ATC codes (such as the bundled WHO snapshot) — no internet connection is required.

The parent-child relationship follows the natural ATC hierarchy:

  • Children of Level 1 "N" are Level 2 codes starting with N (e.g. "N01", "N02", …)

  • Children of Level 4 "N02BE" are Level 5 substances (e.g. "N02BE01", "N02BE51", …)

Usage

atc_children(code, data)

Arguments

code

Character scalar; the ATC parent code.

data

Data frame containing at least an atc_code column (e.g. from the bundled WHO CSV or the output of atc_crawl()).

Value

Character vector of child ATC codes, or character(0) if the code is a leaf or not found.

See also

atc_descendants(), atc_parent(), atc_level()

Examples

# Use the bundled WHO snapshot
data_path <- system.file("extdata", "WHO_ATC_codes_2026-07-14.csv",
                          package = "atcddd")
codes <- readr::read_csv(data_path, show_col_types = FALSE)

# Direct children of statins (C10AA)
atc_children("C10AA", codes)
#> [1] "C10AA01" "C10AA02" "C10AA03" "C10AA04" "C10AA05" "C10AA06" "C10AA07"
#> [8] "C10AA08"

# Direct children of the nervous system group
atc_children("N", codes)
#> [1] "N01" "N02" "N03" "N04" "N05" "N06" "N07"
